+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: d180
+title: Inspector first-field auto-focus only fires on the FIRST inspect of a session
+---
+## Bug
+
+User report: "inspecting isn't reliably focusing the inspector layer — seems to only work the first time".
+
+After the modal-layer refactor in `01KR7CDEFWWVF4WH0BCHE8Y21J`, the first time an inspector opens in a session, `useFirstFieldFocus` correctly dispatches `nav.focus` and focus lands inside the inspector layer. On subsequent opens (close inspector → click another card to inspect) focus does NOT move into the inspector — it stays on whatever was focused on the board.
+
+## Likely paths to investigate
+
+This is a state/ordering bug, not a structural one. Hypotheses ranked by likelihood:
+
+### (1) Layer push/pop race when panel stack briefly drops to 0
+
+`kanban-app/ui/src/components/inspectors-container.tsx:249-253` mounts `<FocusLayer name="inspector">` only when `panelStack.length > 0`. When the user closes one inspector and immediately opens another, the path through `panelStack` can be 1 → 0 → 1 within React's commit ordering:
+
+- Close: cleanup fires `popLayer(/window/inspector)` (async IPC).
+- Open: setup fires `pushLayer(/window/inspector)` (async IPC).
+
+If the IPC adapter doesn't strictly serialize these, the second push could land BEFORE the first pop, leaving the kernel's layer state inconsistent. Then `useFirstFieldFocus`'s `nav.focus` dispatch arrives at a layer the kernel thinks has been popped, and the kernel silently rejects.
+
+Verify by reading `kanban-app/src/commands.rs::spatial_push_layer` / `spatial_pop_layer` and the kernel's `swissarmyhammer-focus/src/state.rs` push/pop ordering. Also reproduce: open inspector A, close, open inspector B — observe if `popLayer(/window/inspector)` and `pushLayer(/window/inspector)` arrive at the kernel in the right order with no overlap.
+
+### (2) `last_focused` state on the parent layer drives a kernel-side focus restore that fights the React-side `nav.focus` dispatch
+
+When the inspector layer pops, the kernel emits `focus-changed` carrying the parent layer's `last_focused`. That fires a focus restore on the React side. When the next inspector immediately mounts, `useFirstFieldFocus`'s deferred `nav.focus` dispatch races the kernel's restore. Whichever lands second wins; the user sees the card refocused (kernel restore won) instead of the field (React `nav.focus` lost).
+
+Verify by adding `RUST_LOG=debug` and watching the order of focus-changed events vs `spatial_focus` IPCs during the second inspect. If the kernel's restore beats React's claim, that's the bug.
+
+### (3) `mountedRef` retention or the `prevFocusRef` capture missing across opens of the SAME entity
+
+`kanban-app/ui/src/components/entity-inspector.tsx:135-187::useFirstFieldFocus`. The hook captures `prevFocusRef.current = focusedMonikerRef.current` only on the very first mount (`if (!mountedRef.current)`), then sets `mountedRef.current = true`. The cleanup resets `mountedRef.current = false`.
+
+If `<EntityInspector>` doesn't actually fully unmount between inspects (e.g. when React reuses the component due to keying or memoization), the cleanup wouldn't run, `mountedRef` stays true, and the next dep change wouldn't capture a fresh prev focus. Less likely because `<InspectorPanel key="${entityType}-${entityId}">` should force remount on entity change — but verify, especially for the case where the SAME entity is inspected twice in a row.
+
+### (4) `firstFieldFq` identity stable across opens — useEffect doesn't re-run
+
+`useMemo<FullyQualifiedMoniker>` in `entity-inspector.tsx:76-83` depends on `[parentFq, sections, entity.entity_type, entity.id]`. If a user inspects entity A, closes, then re-inspects A, all four deps may produce the same value reference, which means the effect's dep array `[firstFieldFq]` doesn't change. With a fully-remounted component the effect WOULD still fire on mount; but if the component doesn't remount (per #3), the effect won't re-run. Same as #3 root cause.
+
+### (5) The deferred `queueMicrotask` cancellation flag is sticky
+
+`useFirstFieldFocus` cleanup sets `cancelled = true`. The `cancelled` variable is a closure-captured local, NOT a ref. On a fresh effect run a NEW closure is created with `cancelled = false`. So this should be fine — but verify that the cleanup of a prior effect-run doesn't somehow keep flipping a shared state.
+
+## Files most likely involved
+
+- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/entity-inspector.tsx::useFirstFieldFocus` (122-187)
+- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/inspectors-container.tsx` (panel stack management, layer mount conditional)
+- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/focus-layer.tsx` (layer push/pop in useEffect)
+- `kanban-app/ui/src/lib/spatial-focus-context.tsx` (pushLayer / popLayer / topLayerFq)
+- `kanban-app/src/commands.rs::spatial_push_layer` / `spatial_pop_layer`
+- `swissarmyhammer-focus/src/state.rs` (kernel layer stack, last_focused, focus-changed emission)
+
+## Reproduction recipe
+
+1. Start the app with focus on a card on the board.
+2. Click that card to inspect → assert focus is inside the inspector field (works).
+3. Press Escape (or click backdrop) to dismiss.
+4. Click a DIFFERENT card to inspect → focus stays on the card; pressing arrow keys navigates the board.
+5. Pressing `s` paints jump pills on board cards (because `topLayerFq()` is `/window/inspector`, but no field has focus, so the pills enumerate the inspector layer's scopes — verify this is what actually happens).
+
+The user's exact phrasing: "seems to only work the first time".
+
+## What to do
+
+1. **Reproduce in a real browser test first.** The kernel-simulator has burned us — write a Playwright/Vitest browser test that mounts the full app shell, opens one inspector, closes it, opens a different inspector, and asserts focus is inside the SECOND inspector. The test must FAIL today (first-time-only bug) and PASS after the fix.
+
+2. **Investigate the hypotheses above in order.** Use the focus-debug overlay live and `RUST_LOG=debug` to determine which is the actual root cause before patching. Don't speculate.
+
+3. **Fix the underlying issue.** If hypothesis (1), fix the layer push/pop ordering in either React or the kernel. If (2), suppress the kernel's restore when the new layer is going to immediately re-claim. If (3) or (4), make `useFirstFieldFocus` re-run reliably on every mount.
+
+4. **Don't reach for the render-time push pattern from iter-3.** That was the wrong fix. Keep `<FocusLayer>` clean.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+
+- [ ] Open inspector A → focus lands in A's first field. Close A. Open B → focus lands in B's first field. Close B. Open A again → focus lands in A's first field. Verified by an end-to-end browser test.
+- [ ] No `spatial_focus failed:` console.error during normal inspect/close/inspect cycles.
+- [ ] Full UI suite green. Rust workspace green. tsc clean. clippy clean.
+
+## Tests
+
+- [ ] New browser test `kanban-app/ui/src/components/inspector.repeat-open-focus.browser.test.tsx` — exercises the close/open/open cycle described above. Must FAIL before fix.
+- [ ] Existing `inspectors-container.auto-focus-on-mount.browser.test.tsx` continues to pass (regression coverage for the first-open path).
+
+## Workflow
+
+- Use `/tdd` — write the failing browser test first, confirm it FAILS today, then fix until it passes.
+- Don't deviate from the task plan without permission.
+- The kernel-simulator pattern is permissive. Use the realistic harness from `inspectors-container.auto-focus-on-mount.browser.test.tsx` as a starting point.
\ No newline at end of file

 After the modal-layer refactor in `01KR7CDEFWWVF4WH0BCHE8Y21J`, the first time an inspector opens in a session, `useFirstFieldFocus` correctly dispatches `nav.focus` and focus lands inside the inspector layer. On subsequent opens (close inspector → click another card to inspect) focus does NOT move into the inspector — it stays on whatever was focused on the board.

-## Likely paths to investigate
+## Status
+
+**BLOCKED — needs guidance.** Implementer (claude-code) attempted TDD per the workflow. Wrote `kanban-app/ui/src/components/inspector.repeat-open-focus.browser.test.tsx` using the realistic harness pattern (`<AppShell>` + `<InspectorsContainer>` + `installKernelSimulator`, same shape as `inspector.close-restores-focus.browser.test.tsx`). The test:
+
+- Mounts the full inspector chain.
+- Opens inspector A (sets `inspector_stack = ["task:TA"]` + emits `ui-state-changed`).
+- Asserts a `spatial_focus` IPC fires for A's first field FQM under `/window/inspector/...` — passes.
+- Closes A (sets `inspector_stack = []` + emits `ui-state-changed`).
+- Opens B (sets `inspector_stack = ["task:TB"]` + emits `ui-state-changed`).
+- Asserts a `spatial_focus` IPC fires for B's first field FQM under `/window/inspector/...` — also passes.
+- Asserts the entity-focus probe shows B's first field FQM — also passes.
+
+**The test PASSES today**, with the contract verified twice (A and B both successfully dispatch `nav.focus`). Captured spatial_focus call sequence:
+```
+[after-open-A] ["/window/inspector/field:task:TA.title"]
+[after-open-B] ["/window/inspector/field:task:TA.title", "/window/inspector/field:task:TB.title"]
+```
+
+This means in the test environment (kernel-simulator + happy-dom + chromium browser-mode), `useFirstFieldFocus` works correctly on EVERY mount, not just the first. Both `spatial_focus` IPCs fire as expected.
+
+## Possible explanations
+
+1. **The bug is environmental** — only manifests with the real Tauri IPC + real Rust kernel + real browser focus events. The kernel-simulator is too permissive: it doesn't model `last_focused_by_fq`, `last_focused` per layer, the kernel-side `spatial_focus_lost` fallback walk, or the cross-IPC ordering that production has.
+2. **The bug is already fixed** by a recent commit (e.g. `b668ccef4` "feat(focus): topmost-layer model — focus and dismiss as commands") and the user's report is stale.
+3. **The bug is real but the test harness misses the trigger condition** — perhaps requires a focus state on the board AT the moment of close (so the kernel-side `last_focused_by_fq` for the parent layer points at a real card scope, and the kernel restores it on inspector pop).
+
+## What to ask
+
+The implementer cannot deviate from the plan without permission. Specific options:
+
+a) Reproduce by hand against a running app build (`cargo tauri dev` or similar) to confirm the bug still manifests today — if it doesn't, close as obsolete.
+
+b) Enhance the kernel-simulator with `last_focused`-per-layer tracking so the test reproduces the bug. This is non-trivial and affects every test that uses the simulator.
+
+c) Skip the failing-test-first step, debug live with `RUST_LOG=debug`, find the root cause, and ship a fix without TDD. (The task explicitly forbids this; the user has been burned by tests-too-permissive twice.)
